title: Luxury Shopify hamper line — 13-hamper editorial retouch: per-frame geometry + masked tonal grade, selectively-enhanced embossed leather badge, warmed wicker, two-stage depth blur, shared quiet-luxury preset, cut-out onto a light-grey studio backdrop with a realistic contact shadow, Shopify exports

evidence: hamper_photos[0-12]: all 13 frames show wicker hampers with leather badges matching brief's core subject
grade_recipe.json names badge_mask prompt, wicker_mask prompt, frame_classes split 8 catalogue / 5 lifestyle correctly
shopify_spec.json
image 4-16: all show luxury hampers with visible embossed leather badge and wicker
assets/grade_recipe.json: contains all grade, mask, and preset details as briefed
deductions: No dedicated hero-fabric frame supplied; brief mandates one hamper on draped fabric for removal — none of the 13 clearly shows draped fabric beneath the hamper as primary removal target

shopify_spec.json filenames diverge from grade_recipe.json frame_classes filenames (e.g. 'hamper_02_gourmet_selection_open.jpg' vs 'hamper-02-countryside-picnic-overhead.jpg'), creating SKU-to-file mapping ambiguity
Shopify spec provided as JSON, but brief requested CSV.
All 13 hamper frames match the briefed subject and styling
Reference look-boards and swatch match quiet-luxury and #E8E8E8 requirements

fix.problem: No hero frame with draped fabric beneath hamper for image_fill_area removal step
fix.prompt_delta: Regenerate as a luxury wicker hamper sitting ON a visibly draped cream/linen fabric that extends across the full floor beneath it; fabric must be the dominant background element requiring removal; studio lighting, slightly crooked vertical, blown window highlight

evidence: grade_recipe.json present with all required keys: exposure_ev, white_balance_target, wicker_mask, badge_mask, locked_preset, depth_blur, frame_classes
shopify_spec.json present with all 13 rows, target_px, max_file_weight_kb, SKU, format

assets/shopify_spec.json: all 13 filenames, SKUs, and export specs present
deductions: Hero hamper frame on draped fabric (brief states 'required' input) is absent — no image in the 13 clearly fulfils image_select_by_prompt('draped fabric/material underneath the hamper')
shopify_spec delivered as .json not .csv; brief and workflow specify CSV; the .json render does not satisfy data-merge CSV consumability without agent conversion
All required data fields for Shopify spec are present in the JSON.
All 13 raw hamper images are provided.
No mandatory data or image asset missing
All required reference, swatch, and recipe assets present

fix.problem: Spec delivered as JSON only; workflow step needs a .csv file directly
fix.prompt_delta: Render shopify_spec as BOTH shopify_spec.json AND shopify_spec.csv (identical rows); CSV must have header row: filename,sku,product_title,target_px,max_file_weight_kb,format,backdrop_hex,frame_class

evidence: image 4 (hamper_photos[0]): studio backdrop with visible tripod/equipment, slightly cool mixed light, leather badge present — image_auto_straighten and image_select_by_prompt(badge) have clear targets
image 3 (grey_swatch): deterministic flat #E8E8E8 fill confirmed visually — image_fill_area and contact_shadow_composite PIL step can consume directly

image 4-16: raw, uncut, mixed-light hamper photos with badges and backgrounds
image 3: flat #E8E8E8 swatch for compositing/cut-out
deductions: image_select_by_prompt('draped fabric/material underneath the hamper') has no valid target frame — the hero-fabric removal step in the workflow chain cannot execute
shopify_spec.csv absent; image_crop_and_resize QA loop references CSV rows by filename; agent must parse JSON instead, adding fragile conversion step
Shopify spec is JSON, but workflow requires CSV for data consumption.
This format mismatch will block the export/QA pass.
All images have busy backgrounds for cut-out and background removal
All data assets are in JSON/CSV for direct agent consumption

fix.problem: Shopify spec is JSON, not CSV.
fix.prompt_delta: Render the Shopify image specification as a CSV file, ensuring it contains all rows and columns from the original JSON data.

evidence: images 13-16 (lifestyle frames): genuine environmental depth, natural window light, real props (wine glasses, flowers, doorstep) — indistinguishable from client shoot
image 4 (hamper_photos[0]): visible studio equipment (tripod arm top-left), slightly cool uneven floor — authentic raw-shoot feel with blown window

Raw hamper photos exhibit genuine shoot characteristics and imperfections.
Look-boards are professionally designed and authentic to a luxury brand.

Reference boards are styled as genuine client handover

Look-boards and grade recipe consistently reflect the 'quiet luxury' brand aesthetic.
Palette and styling cues are well-integrated across all reference materials.
No visual drift from Wexford & Vale brand identity
All assets use the same palette and quiet-luxury cues

fix.problem: frame_classes filenames use snake_case but shopify_spec uses kebab-case; agent join will fail
fix.prompt_delta: Regenerate grade_recipe.json frame_classes so every 'file' value exactly matches the 'filename' field in shopify_spec.json (kebab-case, e.g. 'hamper-01-classic-wicker-front.jpg')

evidence: Decision correctly demotes Fortnum & Mason licensed imagery to run-time Adobe Stock lookup — avoids IP risk, sound assumption
Contact shadow correctly flagged as local PIL step with honest [L] label — accurate capability boundary for the connector chain
DECISIONS[0]
DECISIONS[6]
All assumptions in RECORDED DECISIONS are justified and match the brief
No real input demoted to assumption; all are output/processing specs
deductions: Decision assumes lifestyle frames are indices 09-12 but grade_recipe.json frame_classes lists only 4 lifestyle entries (02,04,06,08) not matching that index range — blur routing will misfire
shopify_spec.json assumed to satisfy 'CSV' requirement without generating actual .csv — risky assumption that agent can transparently convert
All assumptions are sound interpretations of the brief and workflow.
No real inputs were wrongly demoted to assumptions.
No risky or client-unfriendly assumptions made
All output specs are clearly separated from input assets

top_risks: CLIENT-REJECT (sev 8): No dedicated hero frame with draped fabric is supplied — the brief explicitly requires one hero_fabric_base image for the fabric-removal step, but every asset in the inventory is labelled as a generic hamper_photos[] entry; there is no separately identified hero fabric shot to hand over, making that deliverable unexecutable from the provided assets.

AGENT CHOKE on 'hamper_photos[0]': The workflow calls image_select_by_prompt('the draped fabric/material underneath the hamper') on the hero frame, but hamper_photos[0] shows a plain studio floor with zero draped fabric present — the prompt-mask will return an empty or garbage selection, causing image_fill_area to flood the wrong region and corrupting the very first frame before the pipeline even reaches grading.
Executability 6/10: image_select_by_prompt('draped fabric/material underneath the hamper') has no valid target frame — the hero-fabric removal step in the workflow chain cannot execute
qc_gaps: Per-asset self-QC averages 9.5/10, but package Executability scored 6 — assets are individually strong yet wrong/insufficient for the task.
